joke: I told my computer I needed a break, and now it won’t stop sending me vacation ads.
I used to be addicted to the hokey pokey, but then I turned myself around.
Why don’t skeletons fight each other? They don’t have the guts.
Why did the scarecrow win an award? Because he was outstanding in his field.
Why don’t eggs tell each other secrets? They’d crack up.
Why was the broom late? It swept in.
Why was the math book stressed? It had too many problems.
Why did the bicycle fall over? Because it was two-tired.
What do you call a bear with no teeth? A gummy bear.
What do you call cheese that isn't yours? Nacho cheese.
Why did the piano need a ladder? Because it wanted to reach the high notes.
What did one wall say to the other wall? I’ll meet you at the corner.
Why did the lamp get promoted? It was brilliant.
Why did the cloud break up with the fog? It needed space.
Why did the pen fail the race? It ran out of ink.
Why did the golfer bring two pairs of pants? In case he got a hole in one.
Why did the picture go to jail? Because it was framed.
Why did the calendar break up with the clock? It needed more time.
Why don’t stairs ever get into arguments? They always take things one step at a time.
Why don’t pirates shower before they walk the plank? Because they’ll just wash up on shore.
Why did the barber win the race? He knew a shortcut.
Why did the astronaut bring a broom to space? To sweep among the stars.
Why did the sock seem so confident? It knew it could handle any footnote.
Why did the key get promoted? It always unlocked potential.
Why did the button quit its job? It was always being pushed around.
What do you call a boomerang that won’t come back? A stick.
Why was the map such a good listener? It always knew where you were coming from.
Why did the mirror become a comedian? It had great timing for reflections.
Why did the elevator get a compliment? It always knew how to lift people up.
What did the ocean say to the beach? Nothing, it just waved.
